title: ADR-0196: Cancel-on-New-Message — Foundation
description: Implement the cancel-on-new-message pattern for gateway message handling. Goal: when a new human message arrives, cancel any running agent processing for that chat and start fresh with the latest message. This also dogfoods the ADR-0199 knowledge pipeline — all stages should query system_knowledge and emit OTEL.

stories.title: Add message type discrimination utility
stories.description: Create a utility function in packages/system-bus/src/lib/message-types.ts that classifies inbound messages as 'human' or 'system'. Human messages: Telegram messages without the automated headers (🔔, 📋, ❌, ⚠️, VIP). System messages: anything with those headers, heartbeats, batch digests, gateway probes. Export `isHumanMessage(text: string): boolean` and `isSystemMessage(text: string): boolean`. Also export the type `MessageType = 'human' | 'system'` and `classifyMessage(text: string): MessageType`. Include tests in packages/system-bus/src/lib/message-types.test.ts covering: plain text (human), 🔔 header (system), 📋 Batch Digest (system), empty string (system), mixed content with header (system).
stories.acceptance: Utility exports classifyMessage, isHumanMessage, isSystemMessage. Tests pass: `bun test packages/system-bus/src/lib/message-types.test.ts`. Compiles clean: `bunx tsc --noEmit`.

stories.title: Create gateway-handle-message Inngest function with cancelOn
stories.description: Create packages/system-bus/src/inngest/functions/gateway-handle-message.ts. This Inngest function triggers on 'gateway/message.processing' events and uses cancelOn + concurrency to implement cancel-on-new-message. Config: concurrency limit 1 keyed on event.data.chatId, cancelOn matching gateway/message.processing on data.chatId. ONLY cancel when the NEW message is a human message (use classifyMessage from message-types.ts in a guard step). The function body should be a placeholder that logs the message and returns { processed: true, messageId: event.data.messageId }. Register in index.host.ts. Event schema: { data: { chatId: string, messageId: string, text: string, channel: string, isHuman: boolean, timestamp: number } }. Add the event type to the client events.
stories.acceptance: Function registered in index.host.ts. Event type added to client. cancelOn configured correctly — only cancels on same chatId. Compiles clean: `bunx tsc --noEmit`.

stories.title: Add batching window to gateway-handle-message
stories.description: In gateway-handle-message.ts, add a 1.5 second batching window as the first step. Use step.sleep('batch-window', '1.5s') to wait, then check Redis for any newer messages in the same chat that arrived during the window. Redis key: 'gateway:chat:{chatId}:latest_message_id'. If a newer message exists, return early with { status: 'superseded', by: newerId }. Otherwise proceed. This lets rapid-fire messages (corrections, follow-ups) coalesce before processing starts.
stories.acceptance: First step is a 1.5s sleep. Second step checks Redis for newer message. Returns early if superseded. Compiles clean.

stories.title: Ensure system events don't cancel human conversations
stories.description: In gateway-handle-message.ts, add a guard step after the batching window. Use classifyMessage(event.data.text) to check if the triggering event is a system message. If it IS a system message, process it directly without the cancelOn behavior taking effect. The key insight: cancelOn should only match when event.data.isHuman is true. Update the cancelOn config to use an if condition or add a step that checks the message type. Also add a test file at packages/system-bus/src/inngest/functions/gateway-handle-message.test.ts that verifies: (1) human messages get the cancelOn behavior, (2) system messages are processed without cancelling anything.
stories.acceptance: System events (heartbeats, digests) don't cancel human message processing. Test file exists with meaningful assertions. Compiles clean.
